nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated, found over shallow exposed rubble and reef slopes around the Lesser Sunda Islands (Solor, Flores, Bali, Sulawesi, Timor, Ambon); typically reported between roughly 3 and 20 m.

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and overhangs

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied carnivore diet: vitamin-enriched frozen mysis, frozen brine shrimp, cyclops, finely chopped krill or seafood, and high-quality marine pellet or flake food. In the wild, has been documented foraging on small zooplankton and benthic invertebrates over rubble and reef slopes. Multiple small feedings per day are generally advised.

avoidWith: other Cirrhilabrus and Paracheilinus species in small tanks
very boisterous tankmates that may outcompete it at feeding

summary: The Solar Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus aquamarinus) is a teal-to-aquamarine reef fish from Indonesia's Lesser Sunda Islands, formally described in 2018. Generally considered reef-safe and recommended for intermediate-level reef aquariums with a tightly fitting lid.
careNotes: The Solar Fairy Wrasse was formally described as its own species in 2018, having previously been grouped with the closely related Cirrhilabrus solorensis complex. The species name, aquamarinus, references the brilliant teal-to-aquamarine body coloration of dominant males — a hue not shared by any other member of the genus. Endemic to Indonesia's Lesser Sunda Islands and surrounding seas, including Solor, Flores, Bali, Sulawesi, Timor, and Ambon, it has been recorded over shallow exposed rubble and reef slopes at roughly 3–20 m depth. A tank of at least 400 liters with a long footprint is generally advised to give this active mid-water swimmer adequate open swimming lanes. Tank-size guidance varies across sources, so the 400 L figure represents a cautious practical floor rather than a definitive recommendation. Fairy wrasses as a group are well known as jumpers, and a tightly fitting lid or mesh canopy is strongly advised for any Cirrhilabrus species. The Solar Fairy Wrasse is a protogynous sequential hermaphrodite, meaning individuals are born female and the dominant fish in a group transitions to male. In the aquarium it is generally best kept as a lone male or as a male paired with one or more females introduced at the same time. Males display intensified coloration during courtship flashing displays. Its IUCN conservation status is currently Not Evaluated. The species has become available through specialty marine retailers since its formal description, though it is not always in stock and is typically sourced through dedicated reef fish importers.
breedingNotes: Captive spawning has not been reliably reported for the Solar Fairy Wrasse. The species is a protogynous sequential hermaphrodite and forms harems in the wild; replicating that social structure — one male with multiple females — in a large reef system may encourage nuptial flashing displays, but reliable rearing of larvae in captivity has not been documented.

primary.name: Reeflex
primary.notes: Used as primary because the designated LiveAquaria product page (https://www.liveaquaria.com/products/solar-fairy-wrasse) returned HTTP 404 at the time of research. Reeflex supplied adult size (11-12 cm), minimum tank (~1000 L), temperature (22-28°C), origin (Ambon, Bali, Christmas Is., Flores, Indonesia, Maumere, Sulawesi, Timor), depth (3-20 m), diet items, and the jumping/lid caveat.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-verified taxonomy (Labridae / Eupercaria), maximum size (males 9.2 cm SL, females 5.6 cm SL), distribution (Western Pacific: Indonesia), depth (5-20 m), habitat (shallow exposed rubble), trophic level (3.4), and the etymological note that the species was named for its brilliant teal-to-aquamarine male coloration. IUCN: Not Evaluated.

additional.name: WhiteCorals
additional.notes: Cross-checked minimum tank (400 L / 105 gal), temperature (24-26°C), peaceful temperament, harem social structure (one male with several females), and diet (mysis, artemia, fine plankton, pellet/flake).

additional.name: StockMyTanks
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (~10 cm), temperature (23-28°C), pH (8.1-8.4), minimum tank (50-75 US gal / ~190-285 L), peaceful temperament, reef-safe status, and the jumping/lid caveat. Tank-size spread across sources (190-1000 L) is wide but largely reflects baseline vs. ideal long-term housing; 400 L adopted as a cautious nice round value.
